construction.history: represented as one coherent directed family of finite prefix objects rather than a completed finite trace
construction.extension: one uniform constructor maps every requested source prefix and its next-step interface into a compatible target prefix
construction.identity: stable event names and prefix embeddings preserve event identity and order across extensions
construction.dependencies: each finite dependency cone is represented explicitly and commutes with prefix extension
construction.revisions: finitely supported revisions and rule changes retain before-and-after records without deleting superseded history
construction.recovery: one uniform recovery map reconstructs every requested finite source prefix without future-history access or source-specific decoding

reason: The construction proves faithful representation for effectively presented countably open-ended histories with finite recoverable dependency cones and coherent prefix extension. It does not cover non-effective histories, unrecoverable infinite-past dependence, unbounded retroactive rewrites, or future-complete oracle access.
nonclaims: the future-complete history is available at each stage
non-effective or uncountable histories are covered
open-ended history representation establishes a universal kernel
FARA is necessary minimal or unique
